steps.label: 2:30 PM — SPC Outlook
steps.title: A Rare Setup Is Already in Place
narrative: It is now 2:30 PM on May 12, 2022.
The Storm Prediction Center has already highlighted this region for a significant severe weather threat.
In fact, this is the first Moderate Risk issued for this area in many years.
Forecasters are concerned that storms could organize into a widespread damaging wind event later today.
question: Where is the highest risk area located?
How close is Sioux Falls to that risk?
Based on this outlook alone, what type of severe weather would you expect later today?

callouts.text: Click inside the outlook area, then press Details to read the SPC outlook text.

steps.label: 4:30 PM — Updated SPC Forecast
steps.title: Forecast Concern Continues To Increase
narrative: By late afternoon, the severe weather forecast remains focused on our region.
Forecasters are now watching storms to the south and southwest while confidence remains high that severe weather will affect parts of eastern South Dakota and nearby areas.
The official outlook emphasizes a threat of damaging wind gusts near 75 mph, large hail, and a few tornadoes across parts of the eastern Dakotas and nearby states.
question: What part of the forecast area is most important to watch now?
How does this updated forecast connect to the storms developing to our south?

callouts.text: Use this step to compare the forecast concern with the developing storms nearby.

steps.label: 3:00 PM — Live Doppler
steps.title: Live Doppler: Storms Begin To Show Up
narrative: It is now about 3:00 PM, and radar is becoming the main tool to watch storms develop.
This is where students can shift from forecast thinking to real-time tracking.
Use the Live Doppler button to turn on the pulsing sweep, or loop the radar to watch early storm organization.
question: Where are the first stronger storms beginning to appear?
Do you notice individual cells, or signs that storms may start organizing?
What would make you watch this radar picture more carefully over the next hour?

callouts.text: Turn on Live Doppler, then use the scrubber or loop controls to move back and forth through the radar.
